title: PRANA Legal Knowledge Graph — node & edge records
description: Canonical JSON Schema for one node OR one edge in the Legal Knowledge Graph (LKG). The LKG is a property graph (Neo4j-style; see design/legal/graph-db-viz-spec.md): every legal authority is a NODE, every relationship between authorities is an EDGE. This schema is GENERIC and public — it defines abstract node types (Case, Statute, CourtRule, Maxim, Category) and abstract edge types (cites, follows, distinguishes, overrules, interprets, ...). The user-supplied case framework that SEEDS the Category nodes is private data and is described separately by tools/brain/state/design/legal/taxonomy-seed.schema.json (gitignored vault). Every node carries a license tag (licenseFamily) so the AA2-4 license-router can confirm it is PD-clean before display: case texts are public-domain, statutes/court-rules are government works, maxims come from PD sources (e.g. Bouvier's), and Category nodes are the user's own original selection+arrangement (Feist-protectable, user-original).

nodeType.description: Abstract node type. Case = a judicial opinion. Statute = a codified law / regulation section. CourtRule = a procedural / evidentiary / constitutional clause (FRCP/FRE/FRAP/constitution). Maxim = a legal maxim, axiom, or canon of construction (PD dictionary source). Category = a node in the user-supplied seed taxonomy (the protectable selection+arrangement layer).

edgeType.description: Abstract edge type. cites = neutral citation A→B. follows = A adopts/applies B as good law (positive treatment). distinguishes = A holds B inapplicable on the facts (neutral/limiting). overrules = A nullifies B as precedent (negative treatment; includes abrogates/supersedes as subtypes via `subtype`). interprets = A construes the meaning of B (typically Case→Statute/CourtRule/Maxim). applies_statute = Case→Statute application. falls_under = Case/Statute→Category membership (from the seed taxonomy). references = Case→Maxim invocation.

licenseFamily.description: PD-clean license family (mirrors AA2-3 license-tag.schema.json). Court opinions and US government statutes/rules are public-domain (PD / gov). Maxims come from PD dictionaries. Category nodes are user-original (the founder's selection+arrangement). No node in the LKG is ever copyrighted-3p — the LKG holds only PD-clean authorities + the user's own ontology.

id.description: Stable, deterministic node id. Convention: `<type>:<canonical-key>` — e.g. `case:cl-opinion-12345` (CourtListener opinion id), `statute:usc-18-875`, `courtrule:frcp-12b6`, `maxim:bouvier-de-minimis`, `category:<slug>`. The canonical-key is the dedupe key.

label.description: Human-readable name. Case: short case name (e.g. 'Brandenburg v. Ohio'). Statute: '18 U.S.C. § 875(c)'. CourtRule: 'FRCP 12(b)(6)'. Maxim: the maxim text. Category: the taxonomy node title.

source.description: Provenance adapter / corpus the node was ingested from (courtlistener, caselaw-access-project, govinfo, usc-uslm, ecfr, bouvier, taxonomy-seed, ...). Mirrors the BB2 legal adapter ids.

sourceUrl.description: Stable link back to the underlying authority. The LKG ALWAYS links to the actual opinion/statute — treatment edges are probabilistic and must never be presented without this link (see treatment-detection-spec.md).

clusterId.description: CourtListener cluster id grouping parallel opinions.

category.description: Present only when type=Category. These nodes are imported from the user's seed taxonomy (taxonomy-seed.schema.json). The category TITLES/STRUCTURE are user-original; only abstract structural fields appear here.

parentId.description: Parent Category node id, for the category hierarchy (root categories omit this).

depth.description: Depth in the taxonomy tree (0 = root).

seedKey.description: Stable key from the taxonomy seed file used to dedupe on re-import.

confidence.description: Ingestion confidence that this node was parsed/identified correctly (1.0 for authoritative adapter records; lower for fuzzy-resolved entities).

id.description: Stable edge id. Convention: `<type>:<from>->:<to>` so the same relationship dedupes (e.g. `overrules:case:cl-1->case:cl-2`).

from.description: Source node id (the citing / treating authority).

to.description: Target node id (the cited / treated authority).

subtype.description: Refines the edge type with the detected treatment word. For type=overrules this distinguishes overruled / abrogated / superseded-by-statute. For type=follows: followed / adopted / applied. For type=distinguishes: distinguished / limited / questioned / criticized.

confidence.description: Treatment-classification confidence (see treatment-detection-spec.md). 1.0 ONLY for structural `cites` edges harvested directly from a citation graph (e.g. CourtListener). Treatment edges (follows/distinguishes/overrules/interprets) are NLP-derived and PROBABILISTIC — this field is mandatory in spirit for them and must always be surfaced to the user; a false 'good law' is dangerous.

cue.description: Evidence that produced an NLP-derived treatment edge. Absent on pure structural `cites` edges.

phrase.description: The matched cue phrase, e.g. 'we overrule' / 'decline to follow' / 'abrogated by'.

quote.description: The surrounding sentence/snippet from the opinion (the verbatim evidence).

offset.description: Character offset of the cue in the source opinion text.

sourceUrl.description: Link to the opinion paragraph that establishes this relationship. REQUIRED in practice for any treatment edge so the claim is always traceable to the real text.

cue.quote: For the reasons stated, we overrule Whitney v. California.
